heading: Cybersecurity Advisory Committee
label: 665e Cybersecurity Advisory Committee
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of the Cybersecurity Advisory Committee Authorization Act of 2020, referred to in subsec. (c)(1)(A), probably means the date of enactment of the William M. (Mac) Thornberry National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2021, , which was approved . No act named the Cybersecurity Advisory Committee Authorization Act of 2020 has been enacted. However, a bill, S. 4024, entitled “Cybersecurity Advisory Committee Authorization Act of 2020” was introduced to Senate on .Pub. L. 116–283Jan. 1, 2021June 22, 2020Executive Order No. 13526, referred to in subsec. (c)(5)(B), is Ex. Ord. No. 13526, , 75 F.R. 707, set out as a note under , War and National Defense.Dec. 29, 2009section 3161 of Title 50

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish within the Agency a Cybersecurity Advisory Committee (referred to in this section as the “Advisory Committee”).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall advise, consult with, report to, and make recommendations to the Director, as appropriate, on the development, refinement, and implementation of policies, programs, planning, and training pertaining to the cybersecurity mission of the Agency.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall develop, at the request of the Director, recommendations for improvements to advance the cybersecurity mission of the Agency and strengthen the cybersecurity of the United States.

children.heading: Recommendations of subcommittees
children.label: B Recommendations of subcommittees
children.text: Recommendations agreed upon by subcommittees established under subsection (d) for any year shall be approved by the Advisory Committee before the Advisory Committee submits to the Director the annual report under paragraph (4) for that year.

children.label: A
children.text: reports on matters identified by the Director; and

children.label: B
children.text: reports on other matters identified by a majority of the members of the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall submit to the Director an annual report providing information on the activities, findings, and recommendations of the Advisory Committee, including its subcommittees, for the preceding year.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: B Publication
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date on which the Director receives an annual report for a year under subparagraph (A), the Director shall publish a public version of the report describing the activities of the Advisory Committee and such related matters as would be informative to the public during that year, consistent with .section 552(b) of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to any recommendation with which the Director concurs, an action plan to implement the recommendation; and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to any recommendation with which the Director does not concur, a justification for why the Director does not plan to implement the recommendation.

children.heading: Congressional notification
children.label: 6 Congressional notification
children.text: Not less frequently than once per year after , the Director shall provide to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Homeland Security, the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives a briefing on feedback from the Advisory Committee.January 1, 2021

children.heading: Governance rules
children.label: 7 Governance rules
children.text: The Director shall establish rules for the structure and governance of the Advisory Committee and all subcommittees established under subsection (d).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of the Cybersecurity Advisory Committee Authorization Act of 2020, the Director shall appoint the members of the Advisory Committee.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Composition
children.label: B Composition
children.text: The membership of the Advisory Committee shall consist of not more than 35 individuals.

children.label: I
children.text: Consist of subject matter experts.

children.label: II
children.text: Be geographically balanced.

children.label: aa
children.text: Defense.

children.label: bb
children.text: Education.

children.label: cc
children.text: Financial services and insurance.

children.label: dd
children.text: Healthcare.

children.label: ee
children.text: Manufacturing.

children.label: ff
children.text: Media and entertainment.

children.label: gg
children.text: Chemicals.

children.label: hh
children.text: Retail.

children.label: ii
children.text: Transportation.

children.label: jj
children.text: Energy.

children.label: kk
children.text: Information Technology.

children.label: ll
children.text: Communications.

children.label: mm
children.text: Other relevant fields identified by the Director.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: ii Prohibition
children.text: Not fewer than one member nor more than three members may represent any one category under clause (i)(III).

children.heading: Publication of membership list
children.label: iii Publication of membership list
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall publish its membership list on a publicly available website not less than once per fiscal year and shall update the membership list as changes occur.

children.heading: Terms
children.label: A Terms
children.text: The term of each member of the Advisory Committee shall be two years, except that a member may continue to serve until a successor is appointed.

children.heading: Removal
children.label: B Removal
children.text: The Director may review the participation of a member of the Advisory Committee and remove such member any time at the discretion of the Director.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: C Reappointment
children.text: A member of the Advisory Committee may be reappointed for an unlimited number of terms.

children.heading: Prohibition on compensation
children.label: 3 Prohibition on compensation
children.text: The members of the Advisory Committee may not receive pay or benefits from the United States Government by reason of their service on the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director shall require the Advisory Committee to meet not less frequently than semiannually, and may convene additional meetings as necessary.

children.heading: Public meetings
children.label: B Public meetings
children.text: At least one of the meetings referred to in subparagraph (A) shall be open to the public.

children.heading: Attendance
children.label: C Attendance
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall maintain a record of the persons present at each meeting.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date on which a member is first appointed to the Advisory Committee and before the member is granted access to any classified information, the Director shall determine, for the purposes of the Advisory Committee, if the member should be restricted from reviewing, discussing, or possessing classified information.

children.heading: Access
children.label: B Access
children.text: Access to classified materials shall be managed in accordance with Executive Order No. 13526 of (75 Fed. Reg. 707), or any subsequent corresponding Executive Order.December 29, 2009

children.heading: Protections
children.label: C Protections
children.text: A member of the Advisory Committee shall protect all classified information in accordance with the applicable requirements for the particular level of classification of such information.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: D Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to affect the security clearance of a member of the Advisory Committee or the authority of a Federal agency to provide a member of the Advisory Committee access to classified information.

children.label: A
children.text: a member to serve as chairperson of the Advisory Committee; and

children.label: B
children.text: a member to serve as chairperson of each subcommittee of the Advisory Committee established under subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: Information exchange.

children.label: B
children.text: Critical infrastructure.

children.label: C
children.text: Risk management.

children.label: D
children.text: Public and private partnerships.

children.heading: Meetings and reporting
children.label: 2 Meetings and reporting
children.text: Each subcommittee shall meet not less frequently than semiannually, and submit to the Advisory Committee for inclusion in the annual report required under subsection (b)(4) information, including activities, findings, and recommendations, regarding subject matter considered by the subcommittee.

children.heading: Subject matter experts
children.label: 3 Subject matter experts
children.text: The chair of the Advisory Committee shall appoint members to subcommittees and shall ensure that each member appointed to a subcommittee has subject matter expertise relevant to the subject matter of the subcommittee.
